---
title: "Extracting Abbreviations from LaTeX Text"
engine: jupyter
format: html
filters:
  - shinylive
---

This interactive tool extracts abbreviation definitions from your local document entirely inside your browser. No data is sent to a server.

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 700
#| eval: false
import re
import io
import pandas as pd
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive

# =============================================================================
# 1. CORE ALGORITHM & PROCESSING ENGINE (Your exact Python Code)
# =============================================================================

upper_greek_cmds = [
    'Gamma', 'Delta', 'Theta', 'Lambda', 'Xi', 'Pi',
    'Sigma', 'Upsilon', 'Phi', 'Psi', 'Omega'
]

KNOWN_COMMAND_NAMES = {
    'alpha', 'beta', 'gamma', 'delta', 'epsilon', 'zeta', 'eta', 'theta',
    'iota', 'kappa', 'lambda', 'mu', 'nu', 'xi', 'omicron', 'pi', 'rho',
    'sigma', 'tau', 'upsilon', 'phi', 'chi', 'psi', 'omega',
    'Gamma', 'Delta', 'Theta', 'Lambda', 'Xi', 'Pi', 'Sigma', 'Upsilon',
    'Phi', 'Psi', 'Omega', 'sin', 'cos', 'tan', 'csc', 'sec', 'cot',
    'arcsin', 'arccos', 'arctan', 'sinh', 'cosh', 'tanh', 'log', 'ln', 'exp',
    'sqrt', 'frac', 'lim', 'max', 'min', 'sup', 'inf', 'det', 'dim', 'deg',
    'gcd', 'hom', 'ker', 'Pr', 'sum', 'prod', 'int', 'oint', 'bigcup', 'bigcap',
    ' some', 'partial', 'nabla', 'mathrm', 'infty', 'prime', 'forall', 'exists',
    'in', 'notin', 'subset', 'supset', 'leq', 'geq', 'equiv', 'approx',
    'propto', 'sim', 'simeq', 'pm', 'mp', 'times', 'div', 'cdot', 'ast', 'star'
}

def normalize_dollar_spacing(text):
    processed_chars = []
    in_math_mode = False
    i, n = 0, len(text)
    while i < n:
        char = text[i]
        if char == '\\' and i + 1 < n:
            processed_chars.append(char)
            processed_chars.append(text[i+1])
            i += 2
            continue
        if char == '$':
            if not in_math_mode:
                processed_chars.append(char)
                in_math_mode = True
                j = i + 1
                while j < n and text[j].isspace(): j += 1
                i = j
                continue
            else:
                in_math_mode = False
                while processed_chars and processed_chars[-1].isspace(): processed_chars.pop()
                processed_chars.append(char)
                i += 1
                continue
        else:
            processed_chars.append(char)
            i += 1
    return "".join(processed_chars)

def normalize_latex_math(text):
    if not isinstance(text, str): return text
    try:
        processed_text = normalize_dollar_spacing(text)
        processed_text = re.sub(r'\\\(\s*(.*?)\s*\\\)', lambda m: f"${m.group(1).strip()}$", processed_text)
        processed_text = re.sub(r'(?<!\\)%.*$', '', processed_text, flags=re.MULTILINE)
        
        begin_doc = r'\begin{document}'
        b_idx = processed_text.find(begin_doc)
        if b_idx != -1: processed_text = processed_text[b_idx + len(begin_doc):]
        
        end_doc = r'\end{document}'
        e_idx = processed_text.rfind(end_doc)
        if e_idx != -1 and len(processed_text) - e_idx < 30: processed_text = processed_text[:e_idx]

        processed_text = re.sub(r'\s*\{\s*', r' { ', processed_text)
        processed_text = re.sub(r'\s*\}\s*', r' } ', processed_text)
        processed_text = re.sub(r'\s*\(', r' (', processed_text)
        
        pattern_part = '|'.join(upper_greek_cmds)
        processed_text = re.sub(rf'(\\({pattern_part}))(?!\s)', r'\1 ', processed_text)
        processed_text = re.sub(r'(\\[a-z]+)(?=[A-Z][^a-z])', r'\1 ', processed_text)
        processed_text = re.sub(r'(\n\s*){2,}', '\n', processed_text)
        processed_text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', processed_text)
        return processed_text
    except Exception:
        return text

def get_letters_abbrs(abbr_string):
    representative_items, original_parts = [], []
    regex_pattern = r'(\\[a-zA-Z]+)|([A-Z][0-9]+)|([A-Z])([a-z]+)?|([a-z])(?:[a-z]*)|(\d+)'
    for match_obj in re.finditer(regex_pattern, abbr_string):
         command = match_obj.group(1)
         cap_num = match_obj.group(2)
         upper = match_obj.group(3)
         standalone_lower = match_obj.group(5)
         number_seq = match_obj.group(6)

         if command:
             command_name = command[1:]
             current_repr_item = command_name.lower() if command_name in KNOWN_COMMAND_NAMES else command_name[0].lower()
             current_orig_part = command
         elif cap_num:
             current_repr_item, current_orig_part = cap_num.lower(), cap_num
         elif upper:
             current_repr_item, current_orig_part = upper.lower(), match_obj.group(0)
         elif standalone_lower:
             current_repr_item, current_orig_part = standalone_lower, match_obj.group(0)
         elif number_seq:
             current_repr_item, current_orig_part = number_seq, number_seq
         else:
             continue

         representative_items.append(current_repr_item)
         original_parts.append(current_orig_part)
    return representative_items, original_parts

def get_letters_words(word: str) -> str:
    original_word = word.strip()
    if not original_word: return ''
    try:
        command_match = re.match(r'\$?(\\[a-zA-Z]+)', original_word)
        if command_match:
            command_name = command_match.group(1)[1:]
            if command_name in KNOWN_COMMAND_NAMES: return command_name.lower()
        word_to_check = re.sub(r'^\s*\\([a-zA-Z]+)\s*\{?', '', original_word)
        word_to_check = word_to_check.lstrip(' ${}')
        if word_to_check.endswith('}'): word_to_check = word_to_check[:-1].rstrip()
        first_letter_match = re.search(r'[a-zA-Z]', word_to_check)
        if first_letter_match: return first_letter_match.group(0).lower()
        if command_match: return command_match.group(1)[1:].lower()[0]
        fallback = re.search(r'[a-zA-Z]', original_word)
        return fallback.group(0).lower() if fallback else ''
    except Exception:
        fallback = re.search(r'[a-zA-Z]', original_word)
        return fallback.group(0).lower() if fallback else ''

def find_abbreviation_matches(words_ahead, abbr_string):
    abbr_items, _ = get_letters_abbrs(abbr_string)
    num_abbr_items = len(abbr_items)
    if not abbr_items: return [], 0.0, 0.0

    num_words = len(words_ahead)
    match_indices = [-1] * num_abbr_items
    last_matched_index = num_words
    words_ahead_comparables = [get_letters_words(word) for word in words_ahead]

    for abbr_idx in range(num_abbr_items - 1, -1, -1):
        target_comparable = abbr_items[abbr_idx]
        if not target_comparable: continue
        for word_idx in range(last_matched_index - 1, -1, -1):
            word_comparable = words_ahead_comparables[word_idx]
            if not word_comparable: continue
            if word_comparable.startswith(target_comparable):
                match_indices[abbr_idx] = word_idx
                last_matched_index = word_idx
                break

    successful_match_indices = [idx for idx in match_indices if idx != -1]
    count_matched = len(successful_match_indices)
    match_ratio = count_matched / num_abbr_items if num_abbr_items > 0 else 0.0
    perc_words_matched = 0.0; matchable_words_in_range = 0
    
    if successful_match_indices:
        start_range_idx = min(successful_match_indices)
        if 0 <= start_range_idx < num_words:
            for i in range(start_range_idx, num_words):
                if words_ahead_comparables[i]: matchable_words_in_range += 1
            if matchable_words_in_range > 0: 
                perc_words_matched = min(count_matched / matchable_words_in_range, 1.0)

    return match_indices, match_ratio, perc_words_matched

def collect_abbreviations(text):
    pattern = r'((?:[\w\\\$\{\}]+[ -]+){1,10}(?:[\w\\\$\{\}]+)[ -]?)\((?=[^\(\)\,]*[A-Z])([^\(\)\,]*[a-zA-Z0-9]{2,}[^\(\)\,]*)\)'
    matches = re.findall(pattern, text)
    required_columns = ['abbreviation', 'full_name', 'usage_count', 'perc_abbr_matches', 'perc_words_matched']
    empty_df = pd.DataFrame(columns=required_columns)
    if not matches: return empty_df

    abbr_usage_count = {}
    all_potential_abbrs = [match[1].strip() for match in matches]
    for abbr in set(all_potential_abbrs):
        abbr_search_string = re.sub(r'[\(\)]','',abbr)
        aup = rf'(?<![a-zA-Z\(\)]){re.escape(abbr_search_string)}(?![a-zA-Z\)\)])'
        try:
            abbr_usage_count[abbr] = len(re.findall(aup, text))
        except re.error:
            abbr_usage_count[abbr] = 0

    all_candidate_data = []
    for match in matches:
        words_before_abbr_text = match[0].strip()
        abbr_string = match[1].strip()
        current_usage_count = abbr_usage_count.get(abbr_string, 0)

        split_list = re.split(r'([ -]+)', words_before_abbr_text)
        words_ahead = [item for item in split_list if item]
        full_name, match_ratio, perc_words = "", 0.0, 0.0

        if words_ahead:
            match_indices, match_ratio, perc_words = find_abbreviation_matches(words_ahead, abbr_string)
            if match_indices:
                successful_match_indices = [idx for idx in match_indices if idx != -1]
                if successful_match_indices:
                    min_idx_py = min(successful_match_indices)
                    if 0 <= min_idx_py < len(words_ahead):
                        full_name = ''.join(words_ahead[min_idx_py:]).strip()

        all_candidate_data.append({
            'abbreviation': abbr_string,
            'full_name': full_name,
            'usage_count': current_usage_count,
            'perc_abbr_matches': match_ratio,
            'perc_words_matched': perc_words
        })
    return pd.DataFrame(all_candidate_data)

def format_abbreviations(abbr_df, format_type):
     df_to_format = abbr_df.drop(columns=[c for c in ['perc_abbr_matches', 'perc_words_matched'] if c in abbr_df.columns])
     if df_to_format.empty: return "No abbreviations found matching criteria."
     if format_type == "tabular":
         header = "\\begin{tabular}{ll}\n\\hline\n\\textbf{Abbreviation} & \\textbf{Full Name} \\\\\n\\hline\n"
         footer = "\\hline\n\\end{tabular}\n"
         rows = [f"{row.get('abbreviation','')} & {row.get('full_name','')} \\\\" for _, row in df_to_format.iterrows()]
         return header + "\n".join(rows) + "\n" + footer
     elif format_type == "nomenclature":
         header = "\\usepackage{nomencl}\n\\makenomenclature\n"
         rows = [f"\\nomenclature{{{row.get('abbreviation','')}}}{{{row.get('full_name','')}}}" for _, row in df_to_format.iterrows()]
         return header + "\n".join(rows)
     else:
          rows = [f"{row.get('abbreviation','')}: {row.get('full_name','')}" for _, row in df_to_format.iterrows()]
          return "; \n".join(rows)

# =============================================================================
# 2. THE UI & SIDEBAR DESIGN (Replicating your Streamlit Layout)
# =============================================================================
app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.h2("Extract Abbreviations from LaTeX Text"),
    ui.layout_sidebar(
        ui.sidebar(
            # Replaced text area with File Input control
            ui.input_file("file_input", "Upload Article File (.qmd, .tex, .txt)", accept=[".tex", ".qmd", ".txt"], multiple=False),
            ui.hr(),
            ui.h4("Filtering Controls"),
            ui.input_numeric("usage_filter", "Usage \u2265", value=1, min=0),
            ui.input_slider("perc_abbr_filter", "% Abbr Match \u2265", min=0.0, max=1.0, value=0.7, step=0.1),
            ui.input_slider("perc_words_filter", "% Words Match \u2265", min=0.0, max=1.0, value=0.4, step=0.1),
            ui.input_select("clear_duplicates", "Show First Only:", choices=["Yes", "No"], selected="Yes"),
            ui.input_select("sort_selector", "Sort by:", choices=["Abbreviation", "Usage", "% Abbr Matched", "% Words Matched"], selected="Abbreviation"),
            ui.input_select("format_selector", "Export Format:", choices=["plain", "tabular", "nomenclature"], selected="plain")
        ),
        ui.navset_card_tab(
            ui.nav_panel("Selected Abbreviations", ui.output_table("table_display")),
            ui.nav_panel("Formatted Export", ui.output_text_verbatim("export_display"))
        )
    )
)

# =============================================================================
# 3. SERVER REACTIVE OPERATIONS
# =============================================================================
def server(input, output, session):
    
    @reactive.calc
    def parsed_raw_data():
        # Triggers dynamically when a new file is uploaded
        file_info = input.file_input()
        if file_info is None:
            return pd.DataFrame()
            
        # Read the file data out of the Pyodide virtual sandbox path
        filepath = file_info[0]["datapath"]
        with open(filepath, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            raw_content = f.read()
            
        normalized = normalize_latex_math(raw_content)
        return collect_abbreviations(normalized)

    @reactive.calc
    def filtered_and_sorted_data():
        df = parsed_raw_data()
        if df.empty:
            return df
            
        # Handle duplicates toggle
        if input.clear_duplicates() == "Yes" and "abbreviation" in df.columns:
            df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=["abbreviation"], keep="first").reset_index(drop=True)
            
        # Apply standard numerical criteria boundaries
        df = df[
            (df['usage_count'] >= input.usage_filter()) &
            (df['perc_abbr_matches'] >= input.perc_abbr_filter()) &
            (df['perc_words_matched'] >= input.perc_words_filter())
        ]
        
        # Sort values cleanly mapping display labels to data keys
        sort_map = {
            "Abbreviation": ("abbreviation", True),
            "Usage": ("usage_count", False),
            "% Abbr Matched": ("perc_abbr_matches", False),
            "% Words Matched": ("perc_words_matched", False)
        }
        col, asc = sort_map.get(input.sort_selector(), ("abbreviation", True))
        if col in df.columns:
            df = df.sort_values(by=col, ascending=asc).reset_index(drop=True)
            
        return df

    @render.table
    def table_display():
        df = filtered_and_sorted_data()
        if df.empty:
            return pd.DataFrame({"Status": ["Upload a document or adjust filters to reveal abbreviations."]})
            
        # Format percentages safely for rendering view
        render_df = df.copy()
        if 'perc_abbr_matches' in render_df.columns:
            render_df['perc_abbr_matches'] = render_df['perc_abbr_matches'].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.1%}")
        if 'perc_words_matched' in render_df.columns:
            render_df['perc_words_matched'] = render_df['perc_words_matched'].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.1%}")
            
        # Rename columns to match old app layout view
        return render_df.rename(columns={
            'abbreviation': 'Abbreviation',
            'full_name': 'Full Phrase',
            'usage_count': 'Usage',
            'perc_abbr_matches': '% Abbr Matched',
            'perc_words_matched': '% Words Matched'
        })

    @render.text
    def export_display():
        df = filtered_and_sorted_data()
        if df.empty:
            return "No data to export yet."
        return format_abbreviations(df, format_type=input.format_selector())

app = App(app_ui, server)
```
